title: Latent Tyre Health Residuals
summary: Recent F1 tyre-degradation work frames tyre pace as a latent state reset by pit stops, while current solutions only use raw degradation ratios and medians.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre-degradation work frames tyre pace as a latent state reset by pit stops, while current solutions only use raw degradation ratios and medians. A smoothed residual tyre-health signal may capture when tyres are suddenly falling off before the next-lap pit decision.
implementation_hint: Within each Race-Driver-Stint ordered by LapNumber, residualize LapTime against RaceProgress, Compound, Position, and race-lap median pace; add rolling residual mean/slope/acceleration, last-3-lap degradation slope, and z-scored residual versus same Race-Compound-TyreLife bins. Avoid future laps by using shifted expanding/rolling features only.
expected_effect: Should improve ranking of imminent pit laps caused by sudden pace loss, especially when TyreLife alone is insufficient.
risk: If row ordering or synthetic data construction makes temporal groups incomplete, shifted rolling features may be noisy; careless unshifted rolling would leak future information.
